2.q: tet38 transcription increased 20-fold and tetracycline minimum inhibitory concentration (MIC) increased 4-fold in a ΔkdpD mutant.

3.q: tet38 transcription decreased with increasing sodium concentrations by means of direct repression by the salt stress-dependent KdpD/E regulator. KdpE bound specifically to the tet38 promoter.

4.q: Under extreme salt stress, the survival of S. aureus with intact tet38 was reduced compared to that of a Δtet38 mutant. Cell-associated Na increased with heterologous overexpression of Tet38.
